title: Chapter 13 Quiz: Error Handling & Exceptions
description: Test your knowledge of errors and exceptions!

questions.question: Which error type stops script execution?
questions.explanation: Fatal errors halt script execution.

questions.question: A parse error will prevent the script from running at all.
questions.explanation: Parse errors occur at compile time, so the script never executes.

questions.question: What does `die()` do?
options: Outputs a message and terminates the script
questions.explanation: die() outputs a message and ends the script.

questions.question: Which function generates a user-level error?
questions.explanation: trigger_error() (alias user_error()) generates a user error.

questions.question: A custom error handler can catch fatal errors.
questions.explanation: set_error_handler() cannot catch fatal errors (E_ERROR).

questions.question: What keyword is used to throw an exception?
questions.explanation: Use `throw` to throw an exception.

questions.question: Which block is always executed after try/catch?
questions.explanation: finally always runs, even if an exception is thrown.

questions.question: You can have multiple catch blocks for different exception types.
questions.explanation: Yes, you can handle different exceptions separately.

questions.question: What method returns the exception message?
questions.explanation: getMessage() returns the message.

questions.question: How do you create a custom exception class?
questions.explanation: Extend the Exception class.

questions.question: In PHP 7, many errors are throwable as exceptions.
questions.explanation: Yes, PHP 7 introduced the Error class and Throwable interface.

questions.question: What interface is implemented by both Exception and Error?
questions.explanation: Throwable is the base interface.

questions.question: Which of the following is an Error class in PHP 7?
questions.explanation: All are Error subclasses.

questions.question: An uncaught exception results in a fatal error.
questions.explanation: Yes, uncaught exceptions are fatal.

questions.question: What function can convert PHP errors to exceptions?
questions.explanation: You can set a custom error handler that throws ErrorException.

questions.question: Which method returns the stack trace as a string?
questions.explanation: getTraceAsString() returns a string representation of the stack trace.

questions.question: The finally block is optional.
questions.explanation: Yes, finally is optional.

questions.question: What does set_exception_handler() do?
options: Sets a function to handle uncaught exceptions
Sets a function to handle errors
Sets a function to handle both
Sets a function to log exceptions
questions.explanation: set_exception_handler() sets a callback for uncaught exceptions.

questions.question: Which error type should you use for validation errors in your own code?
questions.explanation: For recoverable application errors, custom exceptions are best.

questions.question: You should always display detailed error messages to users in production.
questions.explanation: Displaying detailed errors in production is a security risk; log them instead.
